mnemonics.title: Big O Complexity Order
mnemonics.content: CONSTANT LOG LINEAR QUADRATIC CUBIC EXPONENTIAL: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n²) < O(n³) < O(2^n) - REMEMBER: PLEASE LEARN QUICKLY FOR INTERVIEWS

mnemonics.title: Linear vs Non-Linear
mnemonics.content: LINEAR: ARRAYS LINKED LISTS STACKS QUEUES - elements in sequence. NON-LINEAR: TREES GRAPHS - elements have multiple connections.

mnemonics.title: Data Structure Operations
mnemonics.content: CRUD OPERATIONS: CREATE (insert), READ (search/traverse), UPDATE (modify), DELETE - remember all DS support these basic operations

acronyms.usage: Defines data structure by its operations without implementation details. Stack ADT has push, pop, peek operations.
Principle used by Stack - last element inserted is first to be removed.
Principle used by Queue - first element inserted is first to be removed.
Way of organizing and storing data for efficient access and modification.
Measure of execution time of an algorithm as function of input size.
Measure of memory space required by an algorithm as function of input size.
Basic algorithm structure - receive input, process, produce output.

visualTips.visualization: Think of an array as a row of numbered mailboxes in a post office. Each mailbox (element) has a unique address (index) and stores one item. They are arranged side by side in continuous memory.
Linear: A straight line of people buying tickets. Non-Linear: A family tree diagram where one person can have multiple children.
Draw a graph: O(1) = horizontal line at bottom, O(log n) = slowly rising curve, O(n) = diagonal line, O(n²) = steep parabola, O(2^n) = rapidly shooting rocket.

visualTips.visualize: A stack of plates in a cafeteria. You add plates on top (push) and remove from top (pop). You cannot access plates in the middle without removing top ones first.

analogies.analogy: Data structures are like different types of containers in a kitchen - boxes for stacking, shelves for organizing, drawers for quick access. Each container serves a different purpose.
Time complexity is like measuring how long it takes to find a book in different libraries - one with disorganized books (O(n) linear search), one with categorized sections (O(log n) binary search).
An ADT is like a car - you know how to drive (interface: steering, pedals) without knowing how the engine works internally (implementation).
Static data structures are like fixed-size moving boxes packed before you know all items. Dynamic structures are like expandable folders that grow as you add more papers.
